text_source: We make good time cutting through the mountains,\n
following the path up from the village...
As we forge deeper in, the light from the sun\n
overhead dims, filtered by dense canopy growth.
One of the men Kuon had treated the night before\n
walks at the head of the company, leading the way.
He still has his wounded arm in a sling, but he\n
seems to have recovered enough to walk.
Ukon walks right behind him, whistling to himself\n
cheerfully.
Several villagers armed with farm tools follow after\n
them, joined by Ukon's more properly equipped men.
The whole assembled group chats idly as we push on,\n
hardly anyone showing even a hint of fatigue.
Hhngh. Where do these people get their energy?
Kuon's even been stopping to gather herbs and fruit.
Keeping an eye out for ingredients for her medicine\n
on top of hiking doesn't seem to faze her at all.
Meanwhile, my feet feel like they're about to fall\n
off. I'm gonna collapse if this keeps up.
You all right, kid? You're breathing awful hard.
Anyone would be, after walking all this way...
Really? This is nothing.
Ukon's Cohort
Hey, kid, that cutie's gonna be disappointed with\n
you if you talk like that.
That's right! A real man sucks it up and goes for\n
broke!
Urgh...
I can't even work up the energy for a comeback as\n
the members of the hunting party tease me.
Well, this place is practically a backyard to folks\n
who live here. You're prolly just not used to it.
Guess it can't be helped. Go hitch a ride on the\n
back of the cart. Can't have you collapsing on us.
He's trying to be subtle about looking out for me,\n
but honestly, I don't care about my dignity now.
Thanks. I'll do that.
I hop on the cart without hesitation.
GAHOO!?
What th--
As I climb into the cart, my foot presses into\n
something... gross-feeling.
Wh-What did I just step on?
I look down to find Maroro, pale in the face, in a\n
miserable heap.
He looks so sickly, I can even tell through his\n
thick makeup he's not holding up very well.
M-Maroro? Hey, what's wrong?
I hurriedly pull him upright.
U-Urk... hurk...
Are you OK? What happened?
Oh, alack! Oh, by every god, m-my stomach. How it\n
yearns to shatter its f-fetters... Woe! Oh, woe!
I let go of him promptly.
Gyeh!?
Hwup--aahh. I can finally rest...
Wh-What ill consideration, to so brusquely release\n
me! Maroro 'tis a man besieged, so sayeth--heURGH?
Ye gods, b-but how these backwood trails do chafe\n
the spirit and vex the bowels! Pity this poor soul...
This is why I told you to go easy on the booze.\n
You'd feel better if you walked instead of rode,\n
y'know.
Ukon's tone is exasperated, to say the least.
Mayhaps, friend Ukon, mayhaps--but to debase mine\n
own self so ignobly? U-Unthinkable 'tis...
Oh, for the love of--is this your damn nobleman\n
thing again?
Just so! No man of highest birth should fall so low\n
as to walk upon the dirt, like a vagrant baseborn.
I-It matters--hURGgh--m-matters not the cost! Even\n
fallen from it; grace, we musn't f-forget gra--HURG?
...Yeah, I'm gonna go ahead and say nobody really\n
thinks you're all that "graceful" right now.
To s-sully my very name with such v-vulgar behavior,\n
perish the thoAURGH--HRF--!!
*Splatter*...
Sorry, kid, but could you take care of him?
What, take care of THIS? How am I supposed to--
As long as you're taking it easy in the cart, the\n
least you could do is look after the poor guy, right?
Nngh...
With that, Ukon moves up to the front of the company\n
again, leaving Maroro in my hands.
Bleeuurrgh...
I thought I'd finally be able to relax, but now...
If I abandon Maroro to his fate, everyone will see\n
me as heartless, no doubt.
Reluctantly, I sit down behind the hungover Maroro\n
and rub his back.
All right. Since it's come to this, just... get it\n
all out of your system. Do what you gotta do.
I thank thee, dear freEURGH--
Why me...?
He looks terrible. Here, Haku--give him this.
Kuon approaches with pills in one hand and a flask\n
in the other.
This one is for hangovers, and this one's for motion\n
sickness. And there's water in the flask.
Thanks. You gonna stay and help?
Kuon smiles and steps sharply away without answering\n
the question.
Oh, I don't want the flask b--I mean, um...\n
You can hold onto it.
...I should have figured.
I glance around for help, but everyone looks away\n
in unison.
Urk... M-Master... Haku, was it? Truly, thou art\n
a{W370}--HEURGH--man of g-great kindness, and\n
untold HURK--
Maroro tightly clasps my hands, tears in his eyes.
You, uh... You don't have to thank me, just...\n
point your mouth the other way. Please.
Pray dispense--HURP--with thy humility, O master\n
H-Haku! Ne'er deep enough shall my gr--{W360}AGHtitude--
Th-That's nice, but will you listen to what I'm\n
saying?
Without a thought unto thyself dost thou stoop to\n
aid poor Maroro! Truly, thou art a bosom frienNGH--!
What did I do to deserve this...? How did it come\n
to this?
I groan at the absurdity of the world, rocked by the\n
rumbling of the cart.
